tavily.answer: Recent developments in Haiti reveal a surge in gang violence, displacing hundreds of people in Port-au-Prince. A new wave of gang violence has forced families to flee their homes, leaving them scattered along the road to the main airport. The United Nations reports that 2,300 people have been killed and 100 kidnapped this year, with over 1.5 million displaced. U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres visited Haiti, highlighting the persistent gang violence and the deployment of a new 'gang-suppression force'. Despite the arrival of a U.N.-backed Kenyan-led police force, the situation remains dire, with the government's response deemed largely insufficient.
